goal: To establish a repeatable, rhythmic tempo and loading sequence to prevent static or rushed movements during the swing approach.
To synchronize the hand load and stride to create fluid, rhythmic momentum leading into the swing.
To improve bat control and the ability to execute situational bunts successfully into specific target areas under pressure.
To improve hitting endurance and maintain mechanical efficiency under conditions of rapid repetition and fatigue.
To master the ability to control ball direction by adjusting the contact point based on pitch location.
To reinforce staying inside the baseball and maintaining a direct, extended path through the hitting zone.

drillName: Angled Force Plate Tee - Back Foot
goal: To improve ground force transmission and prevent back leg collapse by strengthening the push-off mechanics from the inside of the back foot.
To reinforce proper balance and prevent the collapse of the back side during the swing.
To reinforce proper back hip loading mechanics and maximize lower body power generation.
To develop a short, compact, and efficient barrel path by eliminating casting or looping in the swing.
To improve late tracking ability and reinforce a direct, efficient path to the baseball.
To enhance core stability and balance throughout the swing, ensuring a controlled and centered finish.
To teach the hitter proper body mechanics and barrel path required to effectively hit low pitches.
To enhance pitch recognition, cognitive processing, and swing discipline by forcing the hitter to make immediate, accurate decisions on whether to swing or take the pitch.
To isolate the lower body and core mechanics to teach the proper sequence of hip-led rotation in the swing.
To enhance elite reaction time and hand speed by forcing the hitter to execute the swing based on immediate visual recognition and rhythm.
To reinforce a flat, powerful swing plane through the middle of the strike zone on pitches at belt height.
To strengthen the lead hand's pulling action and ensure the lead arm maintains a connected, direct path to the baseball.
